steps.title: Identify what the answer must do
steps.content: The sentence sets up a logical conclusion: "Thus, stainless steel used to manufacture ______". The key facts are (1) ferritic steel (used for cooking utensils) has at least 10.5% chromium, (2) austenitic steel has a lower chromium percentage than ferritic, and (3) the 200 series austenitic steel is used for washing machines. Any valid inference must follow directly from these stated relationships.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'cooking utensils will have a face-centered cubic crystalline structure.': Wrong. The face-centered cubic structure belongs to austenitic steel, not ferritic. Cooking utensils use ferritic steel. ✗
  • Choice B — 'mining and chemical equipment will have a concentration of chromium lower than 10.5%, while stainless steel used to manufacture washing machines will not.': Wrong. Both mining equipment (300 series austenitic) and washing machines (200 series austenitic) use austenitic steel, which has a lower chromium percentage than ferritic. The passage gives no basis for distinguishing between the two austenitic subtypes on chromium content. ✗
  • Choice C — 'washing machines will have a lower percentage of chromium in its composition than stainless steel used for cooking utensils will.': Correct. Washing machines use 200 series austenitic steel; cooking utensils use ferritic steel, which has higher chromium than austenitic. This inference follows directly from the stated comparison. ✓
  • Choice D — 'sinks will have similar magnetic properties to stainless steel used to manufacture cooking utensils.': Wrong. Magnetic properties come from the high iron content of ferritic steel. Sinks use 300 series austenitic steel, which has a different composition and no stated magnetic properties. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
